name: Hike of Pic de Soum
region_intro: Hiking around Bedous offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Aspe Valley in the Béarn region of France. The area is characterized by towering peaks, lush valleys, and the flowing Gave d'Aspe river. Hikers can explore a variety of terrains, from tranquil riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents within the Pyrenees. This natural setting provides a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Bedous The most popular hiking route is Hike of Pic de Soum, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route features a significant elevation gain towards a peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Gave d'Aspe – Bedous loop from Bedous, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. It offers picturesque views along the Gave d'Aspe river. Local hikers also love the Orcun – Beautiful stream loop from Bedous, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Bedous is defined by the Aspe Valley, Pyrenean peaks, and the Gave d'Aspe river. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to difficult mountain adventures. The routes in Bedous are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 3,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bedous's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bedous?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes around Bedous, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These trails explore the diverse landscapes of the Aspe Valley, from tranquil riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Bedous?
A: Yes, Bedous offers several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. There are 5 easy routes available, often featuring gentle terrain along the valley floor or riverside.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in the Bedous area?
A: The best time for hiking around Bedous is generally from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the trails are more accessible. Summer offers lush landscapes, while spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher-altitude trails may be covered in snow.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails around Bedous?
A: Yes, the region around Bedous has several trails suitable for families. The village of Lescun, for example, is a great starting point for hikes with negligible elevation gain, often featuring animals, waterfalls, and rivers, making them enjoyable for all ages. Look for routes described as 'easy' for the most family-friendly options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails near Bedous?
A: Many trails in the Bedous area are dog-friendly, especially those in the lower valleys. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or within the boundaries of the Pyrenees National Park, where specific regulations may apply to protect wildlife.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls near Bedous?
A: Yes, you can find hikes that lead to beautiful waterfalls near Bedous. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3123830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanchèse Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area, offering a picturesque destination for a hike.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views of the valleys around Bedous?
A: For splendid panoramic views, consider hiking towards higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877435197/hike-of-pic-de-soum" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike of Pic de Soum&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation gain towards a peak with expansive vistas. Additionally, Escurets Peak (1440 meters) provides stunning views over both the Ossau and Aspe valleys, as well as the Hautes-Pyrénées.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Bedous?
A: Yes, Bedous offers several circular hiking routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6980399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gave d'Aspe – Bedous loop from Bedous&lt;/a&gt;, which provides picturesque views along the Gave d'Aspe river. Another challenging circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24667813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orcun – Beautiful stream loop from Bedous&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in the Aspe Valley?
A: The Aspe Valley is rich in natural features. You'll encounter towering Pyrenean peaks, lush green fields, and the flowing Gave d'Aspe river. The landscape varies from tranquil countryside paths to rugged mountain terrain, with opportunities to see glacial cirques, gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6312141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges de Lourdios&lt;/a&gt;, and even mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking opportunities from Bedous?
A: Yes, Bedous is situated along the historic Via Tolosana (Chemin d'Arles), which is part of the pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela. This offers a wild and adventurous long-distance hiking experience, leading towards the Pyrenean pass 'Col du Somport'.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Bedous?
A: The hiking experience in Bedous is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 380 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Aspe Valley and Pyrenean peaks.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region offers cultural and historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt; is an emblematic site popular with cyclists, offering stunning views. You can also visit the Fort du Portalet, a historical fort nestled in the valley, or the Parc'Ours animal park near Borce to observe Pyrenean brown bears.
